title: sideritis ochroleuca subsp. getula
intro: Sideritis ochroleuca subsp. getula, also known as Getulan Mountain Tea or White Mountain Sideritis, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the genus Sideritis, native to the Mediterranean region. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Sideritis ochroleuca subsp. getula include reducing inflammation and improving respiratory health, as well as possessing potential neuroprotective and anti-cancer properties. Herbal preparations of this plant can be consumed as a tea, capsules, or tincture, often used to treat colds, flu, and other respiratory issues, as well as to boost the immune system.
